tasks: Read the full assignment prompt carefully
Identify the required sections: intro, background, 3 causes, 3 impacts, mitigation strategies, summary
Review the rubric criteria and note what is being assessed
Clarify any terms or concepts you're unsure about (e.g., 'local ecosystems')
Search for credible sources on climate change causes (e.g., greenhouse gases, deforestation, industrial emissions)
Find data/examples of climate change impacts on local ecosystems (e.g., biodiversity loss, habitat change)
Research at least 3 mitigation strategies with evidence
Collect source details (author, year, title, URL/publication) for citations
Organise notes by section
Map your notes to each required section
Write one-sentence summaries for each section to check coverage
Confirm you have 3 distinct causes and 3 distinct impacts identified
Share outline with a teacher or peer for quick feedback
Write the Introduction (context + thesis statement)
Write the Background section (overview of climate change)
Write Cause 1, Cause 2, Cause 3 with supporting evidence
Write Impact 1, Impact 2, Impact 3 with examples specific to local ecosystems
Write Mitigation Strategies section
Write the Conclusion/Summary
Check all 6 required sections are present and complete
Proofread for grammar, spelling, and sentence clarity
Ensure logical flow between sections
Add or verify in-text citations and a reference list
Cross-check each rubric criterion
Produce final formatted version

subsections: Brief overview of climate change as a global issue
Importance of studying impacts on local ecosystems
Thesis statement outlining what the report covers
Overview of how local ecosystems are connected to global climate systems
Cause 1: Greenhouse gas emissions (fossil fuels, transport)
Cause 3: Agricultural practices and livestock emissions
Impact 1: Loss of biodiversity and species extinction risk
Impact 2: Habitat disruption (e.g., coral bleaching, forest die-off)
Impact 3: Changes to water availability and aquatic ecosystems
Strategy 3: Sustainable agriculture and land management
Recap of the three main causes
Recap of the three key ecosystem impacts
Final thoughts on the urgency of mitigation

clarifying_questions: Which specific local ecosystem are you focusing on (e.g., a forest, wetland, coastal area, urban green space in your region)?
Are your three causes meant to be global-level causes of climate change, or specifically how local human activities drive it?
Do you need to include real data/statistics, or is a general explanation sufficient for this assignment?
Are you expected to use formal citations (e.g., APA, Harvard), and if so, has your teacher specified a citation style?
What is the required word count or page length for the final report?
what_to_do_next: Start by choosing your specific local ecosystem — this will make your impacts section much more concrete and easier to research.
Open a blank document and write the 6 section headings from your outline — this gives you an instant starting structure.
Set a research timer: spend 30 minutes finding one source for each of your 3 causes and 3 impacts before writing anything.
Write your Background section first — it's the easiest and will warm you up before tackling the analysis sections.
improvement_tips: Be specific about 'local' — generic statements about global climate change will not score as well as examples tied to a named local ecosystem or region.
Use a linking sentence at the end of each section to connect it to the next (e.g., 'These causes directly contribute to the impacts explored in Section 4.').
For each cause and impact, follow a simple structure: state the point → explain the mechanism → give a real-world example → cite your source.
Even if citations are not strictly required, adding 2-3 credible sources will strengthen your report and demonstrate research skills.
Read your draft aloud before submitting — it's the fastest way to catch awkward phrasing and grammar errors.

quality_checklist.item: All required sections are present: Introduction, Background, 3 Causes, 3 Impacts, Mitigation Strategies, Summary
Causes of climate change are clearly explained with supporting reasoning or evidence
Impacts on local ecosystems are clearly explained with specific examples
At least 3 mitigation strategies are described and explained
The report has a logical flow: each section connects naturally to the next
Grammar and spelling are correct throughout the report
Sources are cited appropriately, or a note is included if no citations are provided
